title: n8n Growth Workflows
blurb: Autonomous research → brief → article → translation pipelines for B2B SaaS teams.

sections.heading: The idea
sections.body: A self-contained growth team in a workflow: researcher, strategist, writer, translator. The system makes content decisions before it makes content.

sections.heading: What it does
sections.body: Ingests a topic + niche. Runs competitor + keyword research, scores opportunities, generates an SEO brief, writes the long-form article, localises it (EN → AR), and ships the artefact to Sheets + Docs as a knowledge base entry.

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.body: Most content teams burn 80% of their time on the un-fun half — briefs, formatting, structure. Automating those frees the team to focus on what deserves to exist.

title: Latency Ad Concepts
blurb: Ad formats that earn the wait during AI inference — without breaking trust.

sections.heading: The hypothesis
sections.body: Every AI query creates a 2–6 second window of pure attention. Today, that window is wasted. Tomorrow, it's the most contextually-rich ad surface ever created.

sections.heading: The formats
sections.body: Sponsored recommendations. Brand cards. Micro-video. Contextual commerce. Each scored against perceived intrusion and contextual fit.

sections.heading: The trade-off
sections.body: Trust collapses faster than CTR rises. The format choice matters more than the placement.

title: Feature Teardowns
blurb: Weekly product teardowns: Rapido, Pocket FM, Uber, and the small UX decisions that move marketplaces.

sections.heading: What this is
sections.body: A running series of short, opinionated teardowns. One feature, three lenses: product thinking, behavioural design, marketplace dynamics.

sections.heading: What I look for
sections.body: Not whether it's pretty — whether it changes a real metric. And whether the cost (trust, attention, friction) is being properly accounted for.

title: Marketplace Sketches
blurb: Two-sided business models with GPS, geofencing, and measurable OOH — mostly notebooks, sometimes more.

sections.heading: The thread
sections.body: Most fragmented industries are one measurement layer away from being marketplaces. Vehicle advertising is one. Specialised logistics is another. Real estate listings are very obviously a third.

sections.heading: What I sketch
sections.body: Supply control, real-time measurement, recurring driver/seller economics, regulatory-by-default. Notebooks first. Sometimes a Figma flow. Occasionally a working prototype.

title: SEO Brief Generator
blurb: A tiny tool that turns a single keyword into a full structured SEO brief in under 30 seconds.

sections.heading: The friction
sections.body: Briefs take 45 minutes. Writers wait. Briefs get skipped. Quality drifts.

sections.heading: The fix
sections.body: A single keyword in → title, meta, H1/H2/H3, NLP semantic keywords, FAQ opportunities, and a CTA. Out in 30 seconds. Editable in 90.

title: Hackathon Experiments
blurb: Weekend builds — usually involving an LLM, a webhook, and a bad idea worth shipping.

sections.heading: The rule
sections.body: If it can't be built in a weekend, it doesn't belong in the playground.

sections.heading: Recent builds
sections.body: A teardown bot that summarises App Store updates. A funnel sketcher that turns Loom transcripts into Mermaid diagrams. A growth experiment scorer.
